etymology.description: semantic 木(木) (mù, 'tree') + phonetic 婁(婁) (lóu) -> building/structure made of wood
etymology.interpretation: The character 樓(lóu) represents a 'building with multiple floors'. The semantic component 木(木) (mù, 'tree') suggests a structure made of wood, referring to traditional building materials. The phonetic component 婁(婁) (lóu) provides the pronunciation without contributing directly to the meaning.
etymology.simplification: Simplified as 楼 by replacing the complex phonetic 婁(婁) with its simplified form 娄(婁).

examples.chinese: 这座楼非常高(這座樓非常高)。
学校后面有一栋新楼(學校後面有一棟新樓)。
examples.pinyin: Xuéxiào hòumiàn yǒu yī dòng xīn lóu.
examples.english: There is a new building behind the school.

examples.chinese: 他住在五楼(他住在五樓)，每天都要爬楼梯(每天都要爬樓梯)。
examples.pinyin: Tā zhù zài wǔ lóu, měitiān dōu yào pá lóutī.
examples.english: He lives on the fifth floor and has to climb the stairs every day.
